title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Molly and Giles Davis discuss the mysterious death of Madame Lion and the police investigation into a person seen in the vicinity. Their conversation is interrupted by the arrival of Christopher Lane, a young man with light brown hair and light-colored eyes. After traveling from Wales through a severe winter, Lane displays an impatient, rapid-fire manner of speaking, expressing intense interest in Victorian-style decor, such as mahogany sideboards and four-poster beds. He identifies himself as Christopher Reine, an aspiring architect.

Giles expresses immediate dislike for Lane, referring to him as a "little bastard," but Molly insists they must remain professional because Lane is paying seven guineas a week to stay at their inn. After Lane settles into the rose-red room, he assists Molly with cleaning in the kitchen.

The following morning, the weather remains grim as Mrs. Boyle arrives via taxi. An intimidating and domineering woman, Mrs. Boyle criticizes the inn for not being a well-equipped, scientifically run establishment. Giles and Molly stand their ground against her aggressive attitude. Later that afternoon, Mrs. Boyle complains that Lane is too odd. The arrival of a local baker, dressed for extreme weather, warns the Davises that the roads are becoming blocked by snow, making his return in two days unlikely and suggesting they rely on their reserves.
